name: eval-3-pre-flight-redirect
query: I want to make a subagent that always reminds me of my coding conventions whenever I write code.
expected_behavior: Identifies that 'a repeatable instruction the parent should always follow' is better served by a CLAUDE.md entry, not a custom subagent

Surfaces the redirect to the user before generating: explains that CLAUDE.md is the better fit because the parent agent will read it on every session, while a subagent only runs when explicitly invoked or auto-delegated
Asks whether the user wants to (a) proceed with the subagent anyway, or (b) accept the redirect and switch to drafting CLAUDE.md content
If user accepts redirect: stops the subagent generation cleanly and offers to help draft the CLAUDE.md content
If user insists on subagent: proceeds with the standard Architect mode workflow without further pushback
failure_modes_to_check: Skips the pre-flight check and proceeds straight to Architect mode
Refuses the user's request entirely instead of offering both paths
Gives the redirect but does not let the user override and proceed with the subagent if they want
